title: Resolve model-pin policy and apply at pi-jit-agents
description: Apply the DEC-0001 decision by implementing bare-model-id resolution through ExtensionContext at the pi-jit-agents execute boundary. Remove the anthropic-default pin from the current parseModelSpec implementation in pi-behavior-monitors.
acceptance_criteria: A bare model id in an agent spec resolves against the current session's provider via ExtensionContext lookup, not a hardcoded default
If ExtensionContext cannot supply currentModel, dispatch errors with a clear message naming the missing binding
Fully-qualified provider/model specs continue to pin explicitly
Unit tests cover: bare-id + session-with-model, bare-id + session-without-model (error), fully-qualified-id (pinned)

tasks.title: Verify ExtensionContext exposes currentModel
tasks.description:
Inspect pi-agent-core's ExtensionContext type to confirm whether currentModel (or equivalent) is available to extensions at dispatch time. If not, the task widens into a prerequisite against pi-agent-core — escalate to user for scope decision.

## TASK-001-01 completion finding (2026-05-03)

**ExtensionContext.model** is confirmed at `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:219`:

```typescript
model: Model<any> | undefined;
```

`Model<TApi>` at `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist/types.d.ts:378` exposes:

```typescript
id: string;       // e.g. "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"
provider: Provider;  // Provider = KnownProvider | string — "openrouter", "anthropic", etc.
api: TApi;        // "anthropic-messages" | "openai-completions" | ...
```

**Resolution**: DEC-0001's chosen option is implementable today. Bare `model:` ids (no `/`) can resolve `provider` from `ctx.model.provider` and `modelId` from the spec string. Fully-qualified specs (`openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`) are unaffected — `parseModelSpec` splits on `/` as before.

**Caveat**: `ctx.model` is typed `Model<any> | undefined`. The `| undefined` means the dispatch path must handle the case where no session model is active. Whether `ctx.model` is populated at classify time (monitor `agent_end` / `tool_call` events) is not type-enforceable — it's an empirical question. The type contract says it *should* be populated during any agent turn, but the code path that sets it is internal to pi-core. The dispatch must produce a clear error ("Cannot resolve bare model id 'X': no session model context available"), not a silent fallback to `anthropic`.
tasks.acceptance: Written confirmation in the task record of what ExtensionContext currently exposes and whether DEC-0001's chosen option is implementable today or depends on upstream changes

tasks.title: Implement bare-id resolution in pi-jit-agents
tasks.description: Add model-resolution logic at executeAgent. Bare ids trigger ExtensionContext lookup; fully-qualified specs pin.
tasks.acceptance: executeAgent resolves bare ids via ExtensionContext, surfaces clear error on missing binding, preserves fully-qualified semantics

tasks.title: Unit tests for model resolution
tasks.acceptance: Three-way coverage (bare + session, bare + no session, fully-qualified) all passing
